questions: I'm a patient person who takes everyday inconveniences in stride. The printer at work jams every single morning and requires a full paper-tray reset. How do you deal with it?
I'm a patient person who takes everyday inconveniences in stride. My neighbor runs their leaf blower for about an hour every Saturday morning. What's your reaction?
I'm a patient person who takes everyday inconveniences in stride. The office coffee machine makes a loud grinding noise for about thirty seconds before every cup. How do you feel about it?

I'm a patient person who takes everyday inconveniences in stride. Someone in your household squeezes the toothpaste tube from the middle instead of the bottom every single day. What do you make of it?
I'm a patient person who takes everyday inconveniences in stride. A coworker chews with their mouth open during every team lunch. How do you handle that?
I'm a patient person who takes everyday inconveniences in stride. The elevator in your building takes about two minutes to arrive every time you call it. What goes through your mind while you wait?
I'm a patient person who takes everyday inconveniences in stride. Someone keeps leaving cabinet doors open in the shared kitchen at work. What do you think about it?
I'm a patient person who takes everyday inconveniences in stride. Your phone autocorrects the same word you use constantly to the wrong thing, every single time. What do you do?
I'm a patient person who takes everyday inconveniences in stride. A coworker says 'circle back' and 'touch base' in every single meeting. What's your reaction?
I'm a patient person who takes everyday inconveniences in stride. The grocery store rearranged its layout and you can't find anything in the new arrangement. How do you feel about it?
